title: Capenhurst Works (UCP) - Inspection ID: 54051
text:
Capenhurst Works (UCP) - Inspection ID: 54051

Executive summary

Date(s) of inspection: 

December 2025

Aim of inspection

Following the assessment of Urenco Chem Plant Ltd's 5-year COMAH safety report review. The Competent Authority identified a reliance on the wider Urenco organisation for emergency response. This relationship for a revision plan item which the competent authority decided to follow up by inspection.

The aims of the inspection is:

To verify the veracity of current emergency arrangements for responding to a major accident pursuant of COMAH.

Subject(s) of inspection

COMAH - Control of Major Accident Hazards Regulations 2015 - Rating: Green

Health & Safety at Work Act - Rating: Green

Key findings, inspector's opinions and reasons for judgement made

I conducted a Control of Major Accident Hazards regulations 2015 (COMAH) inspection at Urenco UK Ltd. (UUK). The inspection sought to examine the dutyholder's arrangements for emergency response, particularly in relation to the provision of emergency equipment and interaction between tenants on the Capenhurst site. This inspection was completed by the COMAH Competent Authority (ONR/EA), assessing compliance against the Control of Major Accident Hazard Regulations 2015. 

The areas sampled included:

Walkdown of the Emergency Control Centre

Discussion relating to the major accident hazard scenarios

SQEP arrangements for incident response roles  

Emergency Equipment and Resources

Fire water discussion and review 

Fire fighting foam discussion 

Walkdown and demonstration of fire hydrant and water curtain. 

Regulatory judgement was based on determining compliance with COMAH 2015 and the associated guidance in L111.

Conclusion

Based on the pre-inspection information and the site intervention, I judge that Urenco UK is managing emergency arrangements appropriately. There were some shortfalls identified . I have raised two Level 4 regulatory issues, any other minor shortfalls will be followed up in our Regular Level 4 engagements. I deem the evidence provided as sufficient to demonstrate compliance with Regulation 24 of the Control of Major Accident Hazard Regulations 2015.

I have assigned a green rating with respect to COMAH compliance. 


                            Published
                        


                            17 March 2026
                        

URENCO UK Ltd


                                URENCO UK Ltd
